clinical_study.brief_title: Ipilimumab/Nivolumab Plus Belzutifan in VHL-Mutated Metastatic Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma (BELZUNIVO-ccRCC)
clinical_study.official_title: A Phase 2 Randomized Trial of Ipilimumab Plus Nivolumab With or Without Belzutifan (HIF-2alpha Inhibitor) as First-Line Therapy in Patients With VHL-Altered Metastatic Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma of Intermediate or Poor IMDC Risk

brief_summary.textblock: This trial evaluates whether the addition of belzutifan (a HIF-2alpha inhibitor) to first-line ipilimumab plus nivolumab (ipi/nivo) dual immunotherapy improves progression-free survival in patients with VHL-mutated metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC) compared to ipi/nivo alone, targeting the VHL-HIF-2alpha oncogenic axis in addition to activating anti-tumor immunity.

detailed_description.textblock: Clear cell RCC is driven primarily by loss-of-function alterations in the VHL tumor suppressor gene, leading to constitutive activation of HIF-2alpha and downstream VEGF, PDGF, and EPO upregulation. The combination of ipilimumab (anti-CTLA-4) + nivolumab (anti-PD-1) is standard first-line therapy for intermediate/poor IMDC risk ccRCC (CheckMate 214). Belzutifan, a first-in-class selective HIF-2alpha inhibitor, is FDA-approved for VHL disease-associated tumors and has demonstrated single-agent activity in previously treated ccRCC (LITESPARK-003). The combination of HIF-2alpha inhibition with immune checkpoint blockade is mechanistically rational: reducing VEGF-mediated immunosuppression may enhance the T-cell-activating effect of ipi/nivo. This study randomizes VHL-altered metastatic ccRCC patients (IMDC intermediate or poor risk) to ipi/nivo + belzutifan versus ipi/nivo + placebo. Belzutifan 120 mg/day or placebo is given orally continuously from cycle 1 day 1. Ipilimumab 1 mg/kg IV + nivolumab 3 mg/kg IV are given Q3W x 4 induction doses, followed by nivolumab 480 mg IV Q4W maintenance. Stratification: IMDC risk (intermediate vs poor), geographic region, and prior nephrectomy (yes vs no). PBRM1 and BAP1 mutation status collected as exploratory biomarkers.

primary_outcome.measure: Progression-Free Survival (PFS) by RECIST 1.1 (blinded independent central review)
primary_outcome.time_frame: From randomization until radiographic progression or death, assessed up to 30 months

secondary_outcome.time_frame: From randomization until death from any cause, up to 48 months
From first objective response to progression or death, up to 48 months
During treatment period, up to 30 months

secondary_outcome.measure: Health-Related Quality of Life (HRQoL) by FKSI-19 and EQ-5D-5L

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria: - Histologically confirmed clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC) or clear cell-predominant RCC (>= 50% clear cell component) - Metastatic or advanced/unresectable disease (AJCC 8th edition Stage IV) - VHL gene alteration confirmed in tumor tissue: VHL mutation (somatic, any type including nonsense, missense, frameshift, splice site), deletion, or promoter methylation, confirmed by next-generation sequencing (NGS) tissue panel or equivalent validated assay; germline VHL mutation (VHL disease) is included - IMDC intermediate-risk (1-2 factors) or poor-risk (>= 3 factors) criteria: Karnofsky performance status < 80%, hemoglobin < LLN, corrected calcium > ULN, time from diagnosis to treatment < 1 year, neutrophil count > ULN, platelet count > ULN - No prior systemic therapy for metastatic ccRCC; prior adjuvant therapy (sunitinib, pembrolizumab) is excluded if completed < 12 months before enrollment; cytoreductive nephrectomy allowed - At least one measurable lesion per RECIST 1.1 (>= 1 cm in longest diameter) - Age >= 18 years - ECOG Performance Status 0-2 (Karnofsky >= 50%) - Adequate bone marrow: ANC >= 1500/mm3, platelets >= 100,000/mm3, hemoglobin >= 8.5 g/dL - Adequate hepatic function: total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN, AST/ALT <= 2.5x ULN (or <= 5x ULN with liver metastases) - Adequate renal function: creatinine clearance >= 30 mL/min; serum creatinine <= 1.5x ULN - Thyroid function: TSH within normal limits or managed on stable replacement therapy - Hematocrit collection required at baseline and every 4 weeks (belzutifan causes polycythemia/erythrocytosis in some patients) - Signed informed consent; patients with VHL disease (germline) must provide written evidence of genetic counseling and consent specific to VHL disease management
Exclusion Criteria: - Non-clear cell RCC (papillary, chromophobe, collecting duct, medullary, translocation, or sarcomatoid-predominant without clear cell component) - VHL-wild-type ccRCC confirmed by tissue NGS; patients with unknown VHL status must have tissue available for testing - IMDC favorable-risk ccRCC (0 risk factors) - Prior systemic therapy for metastatic RCC including VEGF-targeted therapy, mTOR inhibitors, immune checkpoint inhibitors, or any HIF-2alpha inhibitor - Prior adjuvant anti-PD-1/PD-L1 therapy completed < 12 months before enrollment - Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment (corticosteroids > 10 mg/day prednisone equivalent or immunosuppressants); replacement therapy (thyroid, adrenal, insulin) is allowed - Interstitial lung disease (ILD) or pneumonitis requiring systemic corticosteroids - Active CNS metastases: brain metastases are allowed only if previously treated (surgery or SRS) and neurologically stable without corticosteroids; untreated brain metastases are excluded - Hematocrit >= 50% at baseline (relative polycythemia/erythrocytosis is a known effect of belzutifan; baseline polycythemia may increase risk) - Known hypersensitivity to nivolumab, ipilimumab, or belzutifan - Concurrent use of strong CYP2C19 inhibitors or UGT2B17 inhibitors (pharmacokinetic interaction with belzutifan) - Pregnancy or breastfeeding; adequate contraception required - Active hepatitis B or C infection - Active tuberculosis or other severe uncontrolled infection

intervention.description: Ipilimumab 1 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks x 4 doses + nivolumab 3 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks x 4 doses (induction), followed by nivolumab 480 mg IV every 4 weeks (maintenance) until progression or unacceptable toxicity. Belzutifan 120 mg orally once daily starting day 1, continuously until progression or unacceptable toxicity.

Ipilimumab 1 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks x 4 doses + nivolumab 3 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks x 4 doses (induction), followed by nivolumab 480 mg IV every 4 weeks (maintenance) until progression or unacceptable toxicity. Matching placebo orally once daily continuously.
